title: aglaia tomentosa subsp. cordata
intro: Aglaia tomentosa subsp. cordata is a plant species within the Meliaceae family, commonly known as the cordate aglaia. It is a tropical tree native to Southeast Asia, valued for its medicinal properties and various health benefits. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat fever, inflammation, and various infectious diseases due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds. The leaves and bark of the Aglaia tomentosa subsp. cordata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal purposes.
